action.reasoning: Observation: Iteration 18 confirmed the base model outputs attribute-style coordinates (e.g., <point x1="953" y1="7">) ONLY when given the system prompt; without it, it outputs prose. The base model's coordinates are likely 0-1000 grid values (953 on a 3360px image → ~3202 px, which is plausible for a top-right menu item). Our previous base-model eval submission failed partly because predict.py skipped the system prompt for checkpoints containing 'base_model'. Meanwhile, the fine-tuned model may output absolute pixels (since it was trained on absolute targets), but predict.py always rescales by 1000—this would explain the 0.29 val / 0.009 test gap if ScreenSpot-Pro images are larger than ShowUI-desktop images. Analysis: Before submitting anything, I need a 5-minute diagnostic that compares raw outputs from both models against val GT, testing both absolute and grid scaling hypotheses. This will tell us (a) whether to submit the base model with system prompt, and (b) whether the fine-tuned model needs a different rescaling path. Decision: Write and run a quick comparative diagnostic on 10 val samples, then act on the results.
